title: Epidendrum neocauliflorum
taxonomy: Epidendrum neocauliflorum belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a type of orchid that falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Epidendrum is a large and diverse group of epiphytic orchids, with over 1,500 species. The classification of Epidendrum neocauliflorum also places it within the tribe Maxillarieae. This classification reflects the orchid's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other orchids.

constituents: Epidendrum neocauliflorum contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids as its active constituents. intro: Epidendrum neocauliflorum, commonly known as the "Neocauliflorum Orchid," is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of E. neocauliflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
